techniques.evidence_quote: a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key
a database of account names was accessed as a result of the break-in. The database theft most likely occurred in the same October 10-12, 2020 time window.
information from the compromised AWS server being posted on stolen data bazaar raidforums[.]com

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained WildWorks employee Slack credentials, likely via malware or credential re-use from a prior breach, and used them to access the corporate Slack workspace.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an unauthorized user gained access to the WildWorks' Slack workspace through compromised WildWorks user credentials. This may have been the result of malware or the re-use of credentials previously exposed.

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API / Slack Channel
attack_chain.description: Once inside the Slack workspace, the attacker discovered a private AWS access key shared or stored within a Slack channel.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key

attack_chain.description: The attacker leveraged the stolen AWS private key to authenticate to the vendor's AWS infrastructure and gain cloud-level access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key

attack_chain.description: The attacker accessed the Animal Jam user database hosted on the vendor's AWS server, stealing approximately 46 million player records including usernames, SHA-1 hashed passwords, and ~12,000 parent billing details.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a database of account names was accessed as a result of the break-in. The database theft most likely occurred in the same October 10-12, 2020 time window.

attack_chain.description: The stolen database was exfiltrated and subsequently posted to the public stolen data forum raidforums.com, exposing 46 million records.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: information from the compromised AWS server being posted on stolen data bazaar raidforums[.]com

initial_access_vector: Attacker compromised a WildWorks employee's Slack credentials (via malware or credential re-use) to gain unauthorized access to the corporate Slack workspace
privilege_escalation: AWS private key discovered within the compromised Slack workspace channels was used to escalate from Slack-level access to full AWS cloud infrastructure access
